briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for June 29, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴⚠️ Heatwaves and Health Threats Sweeping Across the Globe
sections.content: Europe is grappling with record-breaking heatwaves, with over 1,000 excess deaths reported in France alone, and more than 190 million people exposed to temperatures exceeding 35 degrees Celsius [16, 22, 174]. Experts point out that such extreme heatwaves would be impossible without climate change, and a heated debate surrounding air conditioning, heatwave countermeasures, and climate response is intensifying among European politicians [41, 153, 164]. Domestically, construction workers are experiencing fatigue and decreased concentration due to the heat, and the Ministry of Interior and Safety has raised the national heatwave crisis alert to the 'caution' level [7, 152]. In the UK, energy policies amidst the heatwave have become a focal point of controversy, including orders to remove air conditioners to achieve carbon neutrality targets [42, 87]. This situation demonstrates that climate change is no longer a distant future concern but an urgent reality directly impacting our daily lives and well-being.

sections.title: ⚠️🌍 Climate Change-Induced Threats to Food Security and Ecosystems
sections.content: Climate change poses a severe threat to food security. The nutritional content of key domestic agricultural products such as soybeans, wheat, and milk is decreasing, leading to malnutrition issues despite abundant harvests [1, 9]. The spread of diseases like pine wilt disease is also linked to climate change, creating an emergency situation [15]. Furthermore, a green algae alert issued due to climate change has led to the cancellation of the Inje Summer Festival due to concerns about flooding and green algae, causing direct damage to local communities [13, 29]. The first national ecosystem report warned that while nature generates 34 trillion won in value annually, only 30 years remain in the 'golden hour' for climate crisis response [21]. International reports indicating the accelerated risk of glacial lake collapse in British Columbia and the retreat of the Pine Island Glacier due to climate change clearly demonstrate the vulnerability of global ecosystems [155, 156, 177].

sections.title: 🌱🇰🇷 Domestic Policies and Corporate Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Implementation
sections.content: Domestic and international policy and cooperation efforts to address the climate crisis are actively underway. The Ministry of Climate has established guidelines to support the establishment of a national control tower for climate crisis monitoring and prediction, as well as the introduction and operation of a greenhouse gas reduction-aware budget system for local governments [11, 18, 24, 28, 31, 54, 69, 79]. Domestic companies are enhancing their ESG management by publishing sustainability reports, strengthening climate change responses, and expanding the proportion of renewable energy use [5, 26, 30, 33, 65, 76, 140]. KAIST has appointed Professor Bae Choong-sik, an expert in carbon neutrality and future energy, as its new president, embarking on efforts to strengthen research capabilities [51, 64, 83, 85, 86, 89, 171]. Furthermore, international cooperation continues, with South Korea and East Timor discussing climate change collaboration [8, 17, 32, 35, 37, 167]. However, environmental organizations' observations regarding the need to supplement climate change-related content in the revised Stewardship Code and the potential for certain 'mega-projects' to contradict carbon neutrality goals suggest that continuous attention is required [10, 23, 27, 82, 150].

sections.title: 🌱⚡ Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Challenges Ahead
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is gaining attention as a key driver for overcoming power shortages and achieving carbon neutrality goals. The government has set a target of securing 100 GW of renewable energy through three major mega-projects, planning to support the growth of semiconductor clusters, particularly by utilizing the abundant new and renewable energy resources in the Honam region [101, 102, 110, 113, 119, 126, 148]. Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) has been selected as the preferred bidder for the 800 MW Jeonbuk Southwest Offshore Wind Power project, and Gyeongnam has been designated as the nation's first hybrid hydrogen energy regulatory-free zone, indicating active progress in specific projects [96, 100, 118, 131, 133, 136, 141, 147]. The financial sector is also expanding investments into the renewable energy sector amidst difficulties in the real estate project financing (PF) market, and the development of renewable energy generation forecasting technology using AI is gaining momentum [103, 112, 121, 125, 132, 134, 137]. However, challenges such as pressure on renewable energy unit prices and limitations in power supply for the Honam semiconductor cluster, along with increased greenhouse gas emissions from some companies, remain to be addressed in the renewable energy transition process [99, 123, 124, 142].

briefing.closing: We hope that today's briefing has been helpful in enhancing your understanding of efforts to address climate change. This concludes the climate briefing for June 29, 2026.
